desc: Granada is a Lake Nicaragua, Granada Department base shaped by colonial plaza colour, islets of granada boats, mombacho volcano views. It suits colonial lake stays and rewards timing around the local weather curve, because crowding, room rates and transfer friction shift noticeably by season.

todo.name: Granada islets boat trip
todo.desc: Take a small-boat loop through the volcanic islets for birdlife, lake views and waterside homes.

todo.name: Mombacho Volcano
todo.desc: Use a guided hike or canopy trip for cloud-forest air above the hot city.

todo.name: Cathedral and central park
todo.desc: Base a slow afternoon around the yellow cathedral, plaza and surrounding colonial streets.

todo.name: Masaya Volcano night visit
todo.desc: If conditions allow, add the lava-glow viewpoint as a dramatic evening trip.

wifi.note: Typical visitor connectivity in Granada is usable around central stays, but backup mobile data is wise for day trips and smaller guesthouses.

prac.fltNote: Granada is reached through Managua airport, and dry-season demand plus holiday periods shape both transfers and hotel rates.
prac.currency: Nicaraguan cordoba (NIO); US dollars are useful in tourism
prac.lang: Spanish; English varies outside tourism operators
prac.visa: UK/Irish passport holders should verify Nicaragua entry card, passport and onward-ticket rules before travel.
alerts: Lakefront insects and heat increase in wetter months.
Masaya access can change with volcanic activity and park rules.
Use trusted transport for late airport arrivals.

sweet: Jan, Feb and Dec are the most balanced months for weather comfort, trip logistics and destination atmosphere.
